item.title: Guybrush
item.description: Here I am, back again. The promise to draw more often during the week has already been broken. Anyway, here’s a quick sketch of what Guybrush from Monkey Island could look like — the one I want to draw once I finish my Jim Carrey. The tricky part will be that I won’t have a real reference image to copy the features and shading from. I’ll have to search for photos to use as examples.

item.title: Fat people & hands
item.description: Today I tried to make the most of the downtime during my remote work from home and squeeze in some drawing practice. I spent a bit of time on fat people and hands, to push myself out of my comfort zone and tackle proportions that aren’t the usual standard ones. I learned how important the double chin is, and how key it is to first nail the basic geometric shapes inside the subjects I want to draw.

item.title: Progress on my Jim Carrey portrait
item.description:
Second lesson at the course. I kept working on my Jim Carrey, first fixing the eye spacing because they were way too close. I spent a ton of time on the eyes since they’re super expressive and really characteristic in this photo. I also adjusted the lower part of the face — from under the nose to the chin — because that was too narrow too.

After that I could finally start on the shading: I put down a first light layer over the whole face, then gradually added more pencil only on the darker areas, and kept building it up darker and darker until I got this result. I think I’ll finish it in the next class, but unfortunately that’s in at least two weeks. I really need to make better use of the time between lessons. Maybe I’ll start my Guybrush on my own in the meantime.

item.title: Drawing with Guybrush Threepwood
item.description:
Day by day I’m realizing that to actually feel motivated to draw, I need at least one clear idea or a specific subject to work on. The Jim Carrey face I did last Monday in class is a good way to get back into it, but it’s not enough for me.

I’ve decided my next real project will be Guybrush Threepwood, a character from a ’90s adventure game set in a pirate world with a bit of voodoo magic. So while I finish the Jim Carrey one, I’ll spend some sessions studying pirate clothing and weird voodoo items, which are a big part of the game.

item.title: What a grind
item.description: Since I said I wanted to get back to properly mastering drawing like I used to, I signed up for a drawing course and decided to start with a black-and-white copy of a face. God, what a grind.
